portfolio_rationale: AMZN leads with the strongest bull-to-bear asymmetry — robust fundamentals, positive news sentiment, and a bear case that relies on a manageable liquidity metric. LLY earns second place on exceptional revenue growth and a consistent 3-week bullish signal history, though its elevated leverage and negative news flow warrant a discount. XOM benefits from overwhelmingly positive news sentiment, clean balance sheet, and positive price momentum. GOOGL and BRK.B are excluded due to the lowest internal conviction scores and negative revenue/news dynamics respectively.

AMZN.reason: Strongest bull case with 10% revenue growth, 58.7% gross margin, positive news sentiment, and a bear case that rests on a trivially manageable quick-ratio concern.

LLY.reason: Exceptional 36% revenue growth and consistent 3-week bullish judge signals justify inclusion, though elevated D/E and negative news sentiment cap the weight.

XOM.reason: Overwhelmingly positive news flow (24 positive vs 2 negative articles), positive 5d and 20d momentum, and a very clean balance sheet (D/E 0.15) support a meaningful allocation.

AAPL.reason: Strong profitability metrics and positive FCF provide a solid foundation, though zero revenue growth and overbought technicals limit upside conviction relative to top picks.

GOOGL.reason: Lowest internal conviction scores (llm_margin 0.2, llm_conviction 0.3), no recent news data, and 3 consecutive neutral historical signals make this the weakest bullish case.

BRK.B.reason: Negative revenue growth, majority-negative news sentiment, most overbought RSI in the universe (79.7), and a mixed signal history (bullish/bearish/bullish) disqualify this week.
